front: What is the main purpose of an instruction in digital design?
back: Instructions are used to control the operation of a digital system, specifying actions to be performed by the hardware.

front: Define instruction sequencing in computer organization.
back: Instruction sequencing refers to the process of determining the order in which instructions are executed by the CPU, often involving program counters and branch instructions.

front: What role does the program counter (PC) play in instruction sequencing?
back: The program counter holds the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched and executed, enabling sequential flow unless altered by control instructions.

front: How do branch instructions affect instruction sequencing?
back: Branch instructions modify the program counter to jump to a different address, enabling loops, conditionals, and non-sequential execution.

front: What is an instruction set architecture (ISA) and why is it important?
back: ISA defines the set of instructions a CPU can execute, including their formats and behaviors. It serves as the interface between software and hardware.
